item.title: Proclamation on Ratepayer Protection for Domestic Data Center Infrastructure
item.description: This proclamation states that the United States' economic and technological leadership relies on reliable, large-scale data center infrastructure built domestically. It notes that such infrastructure underpins the internet, cloud computing, and artificial intelligence, and supports economic and national security interests. It outlines objectives and actions intended to protect ratepayers while promoting the growth of domestic data center capacity. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Nomination of Kevin Warsh for Chair of the Federal Reserve Board for a four-year term and for a fourteen-year term as a Governor beginning February 1, 2026
item.description: The White House announced that Kevin Warsh, of Florida, has been nominated to serve as Chair of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System for a four-year term and to serve as a Governor for a fourteen-year term commencing February 1, 2026. The nominations were sent to the Senate for consideration. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Proclamation by the President Designating National Angel Family Day, 2026
item.description: The proclamation designates National Angel Family Day to be observed in February 2026 to honor American victims of crimes attributed to individuals unlawfully present in the United States and to recognize the impact on their families. It notes the involvement of illegal activity and drugs in these harms and encourages appropriate observances and reflection by government and the public. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Nominations Sent to the Senate for U.S. Attorney and Assistant Secretary Positions
item.description: The White House announced nominations sent to the Senate for several positions, including George Kelesis of Nevada to be United States Attorney for the District of Nevada for a four-year term (succeeding Jason M. Frierson); Catherine Dillon of Maryland to be Assistant Secretary of State for Educational and Cultural Affairs (succeeding Lee Satterfield, who resigned); and William Hague of Washington to be Assistant Secretary of the Interior; among others. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.
